rationale.R1.1: The positioning turns on a precise contribution claim ('first PPL in which this decomposition is a language-level construct'); correctly reconstructing the thesis and gap statement—what QuPPL adds that existing systems lack—is central to executing the delegation.
rationale.R1.2: The three-strand comparative argument must be internally consistent: each gap attributed to a prior-work line (no uncertainty decomposition, single-scalar signal, no programmable engine) must coherently connect to the capability QuPPL claims to provide. A logical break anywhere undermines the positioning.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about prior-work capabilities (e.g., 'calibration methods operate on a single scalar uncertainty signal') require grounding in those systems' documented behavior; peripherally relevant since no experimental tables or figures are present, but factual accuracy of system characterizations is at stake.
rationale.R1.4: This is the entire delegation—assessing the novelty delta between QuPPL and its nearest neighbors across general-purpose PPLs, calibration/selective-prediction, and Bayesian-semantics lines. Failure to correctly identify or bound this delta invalidates the output.
rationale.R1.7: The positioning is only as strong as the coverage and attribution of the prior-work landscape. The artifact names three lines but cites no specific works; gaps in coverage or misattribution of capabilities to those lines would hollow out the novelty claims.
